items.title: I used the Pixel 10a and OnePlus 15R, and one feels like a total steal
items.summary: It’s a trap we all fall into, regardless of what we’re buying. If I spend just a little bit more, will I get a better product?

items.title: I let Google's Call Assistant handle my phone for a week to see if any real callers got through
items.summary: "I don't like spam callers" might be the coldest, most uncontroversial take of all time, but it was heavy on my mind a few weeks ago.

items.title: I never lose my work now, thanks to this brilliant app that saves my texts
items.summary: My favorite unofficial category on the Play Store is "apps that look like they're stuck in 2014."

items.title: I let Android Routines take over my morning, and I'm never going back to my phone's alarm app
items.summary: As a heavy sleeper, I've always been at the mercy of alarm clocks. My young, slumbering mind required the loud beeps and incessant radio chatter to jolt me from my bed, lest I miss out on the joys of first period.

items.title: I replaced my $100 webcam with my Android's built-in option, and my video calls look twice as crisp
items.summary: Most of us spend at least a portion of our working week on video calls, and the reality of video calls is that improving your video call quality invariably requires buying better hardware.

items.title: I used Gemini's image analysis on my phone for a week, and it ruined Google Lens for me
items.summary: I have relied on Google Lens since I bought my first Xiaomi smartphone in 2016, and it has been an essential feature in every device I have owned.

items.title: 6 Android automation features already on your phone that most people do not know exist
items.summary: If you're deep into the Android ecosystem, you may think that automation requires apps like Tasker or complicated routines, perhaps even our spend-tweaking settings.

items.title: I deleted most Android Auto apps, but only 6 earned a permanent spot on my dashboard
items.summary: When I first started using Android Auto regularly, I downloaded way too many apps for it.

items.title: How a simple 2-week Android experiment changed the way I interact with social media
items.summary: It's been a long time since an Android update excited me, though I'm fine with that. It gives me more time to visit every nook and corner of the operating system, especially the ones I've ignored all my life.

items.title: Gemini is about to change how you find Android apps, and it's actually useful
items.summary: Finding a good Android app shouldn't be difficult. Yet you've probably searched for an app before and had to scroll past ads, broken trust signals , and knockoffs before finding something worth installing.
